items.headline: Major commercial development approved on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial project is moving forward at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant business investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest permit issued in this batch and signals continued economic development in the area.

items.headline: City commissions unveil priorities for next fiscal year
items.summary: Saratoga's five commissions—covering parks, planning, traffic safety, heritage preservation, and libraries—presented their work plans to the City Council for fiscal year 2026-27. The work plans outline what projects and initiatives each commission will prioritize over the coming year.

items.headline: Planning Commission work plan includes zoning and development review
items.summary: The Planning Commission's FY 2026-27 work plan was approved, which typically covers review of new development projects, zoning changes, and land-use decisions that directly affect what gets built in Saratoga and where residents can expect changes to their neighborhoods.

items.headline: Traffic Safety Commission focuses on road safety improvements
items.summary: The Traffic Safety Commission presented its work plan priorities for the coming year, likely including pedestrian safety, intersection improvements, and traffic calming measures on local streets that affect daily commutes and neighborhood safety.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses get licensed and participate in outreach initiatives. This could affect local shopping centers and small businesses looking to formalize their operations.

items.headline: City council approves new economic incentives for retail and startups
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing incentive programs to attract and support retail businesses and startups. The council also approved sponsorships for summer community events through public-private partnerships.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: City Council approved making the Great American Litter Pick-up an official annual city-sponsored special event, with authorization to spend city funds and accept material donations. This expands the city's community cleanup efforts.

items.headline: City reviews federal housing grant funding applications
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25, which provides federal money for affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. These grants directly impact what housing and service programs the city can afford to implement.
